feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Apps
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Apps Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build App request destination 401
entry.content:
Hi Experts:

I create a SAP Build APP, but trigger process failed for 401

in preview will return 401 to 

{
"status": 401,
"statusText": "Unauthorized",
"headers": {
"cache-control": "no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate",
"expires": "0",
"pragma": "no-cache",
"referrer-policy": "no-referrer",
"www-authenticate": "Bearer error=\"invalid_token\", error_description=\"Error initializing JWT decoder: The token is invalid: Jwt token with audience [destination-xsappname!b9.instance, sb-cloneb3a595c52ed142ec86069eecbc4be8b3!b7280|destination-xsappname!b9, uaa, openid, xs_account, destination-xsappname!b9.subaccount, destination-xsappname!b9] is not issued for these clientIds: [sb-xsuaa!b3591, xsuaa!b3591].\", error_uri=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-3.1\"",
"x-content-type-options": "nosniff",
"x-frame-options": "DENY",
"x-vcap-request-id": "c09776e6-8d3a-4d72-73ec-24a760e9b37b",
"x-xss-protection": "0",
"date": "Fri, 30 Jan 2026 06:45:24 GMT",
"strict-transport-security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload;",
"connection": "close"
},
"responseText": ""
}
my destination is

in postman, use token get from the clientId and secret in destination, I can call my process successfully

is there someone can help me, thanks a lot!

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

it can work now, maybe it's because there's some delay with the server.

SAP Build SAP Build Apps SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Is SAP Build subscription alone sufficient to create mobile applications, or is SAP Build Apps subsc
entry.content:
As per SAP documentation, SAP Build is a bundle that includes the following services:

  1. SAP Build Apps

  2. SAP Build Process Automation

  3. SAP Build Work Zone

I would like to clarify the licensing and subscription aspect.

If we subscribe only to SAP Build, is that sufficient to create and deploy mobile applications using SAP Build Apps?

Or do we need to purchase/subscribe to SAP Build Apps separately, even though it is part of the SAP Build bundle?

It would be helpful if someone could explain:

Whether SAP Build Apps is automatically included with SAP Build

Or if it requires an individual subscription/entitlement

Any limitations (users, runtime, mobile deployment) with SAP Build Apps under SAP Build

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: BPA Unable to verify the signature of the archive (Transport Joule Skills)
entry.content:
Hi guys, could anyone help me out?

In SAP Build Process Automation (BPA), I’m trying to migrate/transport a Joule Skill project from Subaccount A (US, Azure) to Target Subaccount B (another region, US VA, AWS).

The import fails with this error: 

Unable to verify the signature of the archive. Make sure the landscape keys from landscape us21-prod are imported in your landscape, if not, contact DevOps. Ensure that the other potential reasons for this error are checked. This error could happen for the following reasons: Couldn't get key xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx version from credential store.

The same error - Transport and Upload proyect.

 

I’ve searched everywhere and couldn’t find anything useful.

I did find an SAP Note that suggests using a tenant in the same region, which is honestly very limiting because it would force us to change our entire landscape design just because of BPA:

 

 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3472593/E

Has anyone found a way to solve this (e.g., importing landscape keys / fixing credential store key access) and successfully transport BPA/Joule projects across regions?

I already opened a ticket with SAP, but I haven’t received an answer yet.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Clarification on SAP Build Apps Subscription Visibility under build-default Plan
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We need your clarification regarding SAP Build Apps subscription visibility in our SAP BTP subaccount.

Currently, our subaccount has the following subscriptions active under the build-default plan:

SAP Build Process Automation – build-default

SAP Business Application Studio – build-default

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – build-default

As per SAP documentation and licensing understanding, SAP Build Apps is included within the build-default plan and does not appear as a standalone subscription.

However, when we attempt to create a project using Create Project → Web Application (low-code) in SAP Build Apps, the system displays the status as “Not Subscribed”, which creates confusion from an entitlement perspective.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether SAP Build Apps entitlement is correctly provided through the build-default plan in our subaccount, and

  2. Why the project creation wizard displays “Not Subscribed” despite the build-default plan being active.

Your confirmation will help us proceed and also communicate the correct information to our stakeholders.

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: BTP BuildApps- Error handling and logging possibility
entry.content:
Hello community,

Currently,  we are developing Build Apps as mashup/extension for Sales and Service Cloud V2 or C4C V2.

Now we have use case to handle the address validation logic in Build Apps where the external APIs are consumed, that clearly means the APIs could give us different errors/exceptions in case any issue occurred from external system. How can we log and monitor errors occurred in Build Apps from external systems ?

Examples:
Network & Connectivity Errors: Timeouts, DNS failures, and connection resets.
Authentication/Authorization Errors: 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, invalid or expired API keys

Rate Limits and Quotas: Exceeding allowed request volume
Data Format/Schema Mismatches: API changes its data structure
API Provider Downtime (5xx Errors): 500 Internal Server Error (server crash) or 503 Service Unavailable (server overload)
Invalid URL/Endpoint Changes: The provider updates their documentation or removes an endpoint without notice

Can you guide us how to handle errors in Build Apps so that we can take advantage of logging and monitoring?
Thank you.
Willem_Pardaens @vvdries @gregorw @qmacro @martinstenzig

entry.title: Automation around Transports (STMS_IMPORT)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am wondering whether somebody from community has "good" experience with automation transport requests in SAP. I am talking about technical transports requests (STMS), not logistics.

In the past I build a process automation with a form where selected users could enter a transport request. The bot checked that request for some preconditions and imported it then into a SAP system.

In the meantime and with much more experience I doubt whether this scenario is really good for a "bot". The check of the preconditions is really complex, because the request is only allowed to have special objects included and nothing else. I am importing with BAPI. There I only can start the import but don't get a reliable feedback (especially for huge requests). Also there are no BAPI for STMS_QA, so I don't get this list clean.

I also noticed that SAP itself doesn't have any automation around STMS_IMPORT in her portfolio (store). That must also have some reasons because there are over 600 projects included and STMS uses nearly every customer.

In the past we dealt that topic with tickets and a manual import from "IT guys".

What are your thoughts about that issue?

entry.title: I want to Create a REST Data Entity using BTP Destination. but unable retrieve expected output.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want to create a REST Data Entity from my build apps workspace for 'API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV' (/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/) odata service.

I have a BTP destination already configured.

1.Adding BTP Destination in Base section2. adding odata path in the retrieve section3.Test the data entity. got200

4.But not receiving the expected databut when I am retrieving data I am getting below result, it returns status -200. which is OK.

 

But I am not at all expecting this response. It is returning bunch of standard available catalog details. so something is wrong here.

 

Can someone please guide me what is going wrong here.

Is there any specific way I should configure my BTP Destination?

entry.title: Error while accessing camera app from SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Recenlty we developed one application in SAP Build Apps having QR code scan and Take Photo flow function. This application works well in Preview mode.

Once we deployed it in Build Work zone and then accessing it through mobile start application in mobile device, 'Scan QR code' function is working fine, but 'Take Photo' function is not able to access the camera.

Getting below error:

platformNotSupported : This platform is not supported

{"code":"platformNotSupported","platform":"web"}

Looking for your expert advice on this.

SAP Build Apps , SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition , Joule Work mobile app for iOS

entry.title: SBPA Destination Error: Destination not found.
entry.content:
I am following below tutorial from developer.sap.com and getting destination connectivity issue.
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

{error: "Error: Destination not found.↵", message: "Destination not found."}
error: "Error: Destination not found.\n" 
message: "Destination not found."

Steps

1. I have completed Create a Sales Order Process Using SAP Build Process Automation Successfully.

2. After that I have created Create a Sales Order App Using SAP Build Apps from SAP Build Successfully.

3. When I try to access from Build App Preview getting above destination issue.

4. As per tutorial it will work like this.

Regards,

Abderrahmane Kermiche

entry.title: 401 Unauthorized - Build Apps Unable to Access BTP Destination API
entry.content:
I am facing strange error while calling SAP Build process automation from SAP Build apps using SAP Build apps published library API integration in which i have published my build process automation API trigger.

Performed all steps correctly in steps provided in https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

When i debugged the issue i found the error as below

entry.title: Request to Preserve AppGyver’s Legacy: Consider Open‑Sourcing the Pre‑SAP Codebase
entry.content:
I've been a forever fan of Appgyver, sorry in SAP terms SAP Build Apps. Frankly, I saw it with some sadness that the offering is now in deprecation. However, it wasn't really unexpected. The decline of dedicated no‑code tools in the age of LLM‑assisted code generation is understandable. The speed and volume of code agentic coding can deliver does reduce the perceived commercial need for highly visual builders. However, AppGyver’s value was never limited to “just” being a tool; it was a gentle on‑ramp for non‑experts, a playground for experimentation, and a platform that required relatively little investment to become a long‑lasting, sustainable project.

Beyond the technology, there was also a clear sense of intellect and ethos in the original AppGyver team. The openness to community feedback, a commitment to empowering non‑developers, and a design‑first mindset that honestly feels harder to find in large enterprise environments. Seeing that spirit fade into the more rigid structures of a major corporate ecosystem is, frankly, a cultural loss as much as a technical one.

I fully understand that fully open‑sourcing an SAP‑owned product such as SAP Build Apps (the successor of AppGyver) is unlikely to align with SAP’s policies or strategic direction. However, I would like to respectfully propose a more modest and symbolic ask: consider open‑sourcing the AppGyver codebase as it existed prior to the SAP acquisition.

Naturally, following the acquisition the product evolved tremendously, and became an integral part of SAP. I still am convinced, however, the pre‑SAP state of AppGyver still represents an important milestone in professional no‑code development. Releasing that codebase (even if clearly marked as historical artifact) would: 

  • Preserve the intellectual and technical legacy of a pioneering no‑code platform.

  • Allow the community, researchers, and educators to study and learn from its architecture.

  • Enable interested individuals or small groups to invest time and effort into maintaining or evolving it independently, if they choose.

  • Signal that SAP values the early no‑code community and its contributions, even if the formal product line is being retired.

The release of the pre‑SAP code as open source would feel like a more humane and forward‑looking gesture from SAP, rather than just another deprecation, especially given the unexpected nature of this one. It would clearly not bind SAP to continued maintenance, but it would more likely honor the spirit of the original AppGyver project and give its knowledge a second life in the wider developer ecosystem.

I appreciate the work SAP has put in the low/no-code space over the past years, and thank you for taking the time to consider this request.

entry.title: Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)
entry.content:
Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)

When building applications using SAP Build Apps, a common requirement is to trigger workflows created in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).

However, many developers face confusion:

Can we trigger a Form-based process directly from Build Apps?

👉 Short answer: No — Build Apps can only trigger workflows via API.
But don’t worry — there’s a proper workaround.

In this blog, we’ll cover:

  • ✅ Triggering API-based workflows
  • ✅ Triggering Form-based workflows via API workaround
  • ✅ End-to-end configuration

Understanding the Problem

In SAP Build Process Automation:

Trigger TypeCan Build Apps Trigger Directly?
Form TriggerNo
API TriggerYes

Reason:
Build Apps communicates via REST APIs, not UI forms.

ℹ️To Integrate Build Apps with Process Automation we need BTP Destination
Create Destination in SAP BTP with the same Name "sap_process_automation_service_user_access". Authentication can be either OAuth2ClientCredentials or OAuth2JWTBearer but add the correct Client ID and Client Secret ID from Service key from the instance of SAP Build Process Automation. Additional Properties are important to get the success integration. Finally save and check the connection.

Approach 1: API-Based Workflow Trigger 

Step 1: Create Process with API Trigger

  • Open your BPA project
  • Create a Process
  • Select Start Event → API Trigger

    Define Input Schema:
    {
    "FirstName": "string"
    }

Step 2: Deploy the Process

Click the three dots in SAP lobby of deployed process and publish to library. So that only you can integrate deployed process in SAP Build Apps.

After deployment, you will get:

  • Workflow API endpoint
  • Process Definition ID

Step 3: Configure SAP Build Apps

In SAP Build Apps:

1.Go to App Settings

Click Enable Authentication, Choose SAP BTP Authentication. Click Ok on the Popup.
If already SAP BTP Authentication added in App Setting Leave the Above Step

2.Integrate Workflow Process

Go to Integrations Tab Click Add Integration Button, Choose SAP Build Library. Under Processes you can select the published workflow process and  Enable process

3.Create UI

  1. Input fields:
    -> FirstName
  2. Start Process Button
    Add Logic to the above Button.

In Logic window, Add Trigger Process, choose the enabled Process and configure the input parameter as object with properties. To show status you can add either Alert/ Toast Message.
     

✅ Result

  • User give inputs in Build Apps
  • Clicks Submit
  • Workflow starts in BPA

Approach 2: Form-Based Process 

⚠️ Important Concept

Even if your process uses a Form Start Event,
👉 there is NO direct API to submit that form

So this will NOT work:
❌ Build Apps → Submit BPA Form directly

✅ Correct Workaround

🔄Convert Form Input → API Payload in SAP Build Apps

Step 1: Create Form Based Trigger Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.

    • Here I took Leave Approval Application Workflow and deploy it. Trigger the process once then you can see the definition Id in Monitoring tab for this triggered workflow. Copy that ID for later use.
        

Step 2 : Create Build Apps Application 

  • Add SAP BTP Authentication in App Settings if not you not done yet.
  • Go to Integration tab and create a data entity by selecting SAP BTP Destination Rest API integration.
    • In Base, Choose the destination and add resource schema. ⚠️ Do not add common request header for token passing

    • Click on create and Enable it.
      • Set the relative path and query - Add static value "/public/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances".
      • Set Request Method as POST. 
      • Add Request Headers as List -> Key : Content-Type, Value :  application/json.
      • Add Request Body Mapper by wrap the data as json. and Save the data entity.
        ENCODE_JSON({"definitionId":"<value of the definitionId which is copied from Monitoring tab in SAP BPA>", "context": {<all parameters present in startEvent of input schema (you can refer in monitoring tab of the triggered process>}})


  • Create UI with required input fields. Add Button and configure the process flow in logic canvas.
    • Add create record and configure the resource which is create by following above steps. Set the records as object with properties. Add Toast Message/ Alert for your purpose.
    • Test the App, you will get the result and also you can cross check with monitoring tab in SAP BPA for the triggered process

⚠️ Common Issues

❌ Invalid Token 

  • Wrong OAuth configuration
  • No proper Addition Properties

❌ 401 Unauthorized

  • Client ID / Secret issue

❌ Workflow Not Starting

  • Wrong definitionID
  • Incorrect payload format

These can resolve by adding correct input configuration and Correct destination configuration.

🔄 Architecture Overview

SAP Build Apps (UI Form) ➡️ REST API Call (OAuth) ➡️ SAP Build Process Automation ➡️ Workflow Execution
 
🎯 Best Practices
✔ Validate payload before sending
✔ Use destinations for secure connectivity
✔ Always use inspect to check the console for error

Conclusion

🎉 And that’s a wrap!
You’ve now unlocked the power of triggering workflows in SAP Build Process Automation directly from SAP Build Apps using API-driven integration.
What started as a simple requirement—triggering a workflow—has evolved into a powerful architectural pattern:

  • 🎯 Build Apps handling the user experience
  • ⚙️ SAP BPA orchestrating the process logic
  • 🔗 APIs acting as the bridge between them

By moving beyond Form Triggers and embracing an API-first approach, you’re not just solving a limitation—you’re designing a solution that is scalable, flexible, and enterprise-ready.

💡 Imagine the possibilities:
From employee requests and approvals to complex cross-system automations—this pattern can power countless real-world scenarios with efficiency and control.

🚀 And this is just the beginning.
You can take it further by integrating additional services, enhancing validations, or building intelligent, event-driven workflows across your SAP landscape.

If you found this helpful or have ideas to expand this approach, feel free to connect and share your thoughts!

Let’s build smarter automation together.

LinkedIn : https://www.linkedin.com/in/abirami-t/

entry.title: Send Email Activity in SAP iRPA giving error
entry.content:
I was sending email via send email activity, it was working since last 6 months. Today it showed an error- SAP irpa core error - Failed to send email Outlook does not recognize one or more names. I can't understand what's wrong here.
Can someone please help me!!!!

entry.title: Exporting SAP Build Apps: Converting "Web and Mobile" to "Web Only" for Build-Default Plan
entry.content:
Hello,

I am moving my development work between tenants. My original project was created as a "Web and Mobile Application" in a tenant with a Free Plan. My new tenant is on the Build-Default plan.

When attempting to import my project, I am blocked by the issue described in KBA 3693320, as the Build-Default plan does not support "Web and Mobile" project types.

Since there is no "Convert to Web-Only" button in the UI, I am looking for the most efficient way to take my work with me.

I would like to know:

  1. Is there any way to export the project so that the target tenant accepts it as a "Web-Only" app?

  2. If direct import is impossible, is there a supported (or community-tested) way to copy objects, logic, or pages from the "Web and Mobile" project and paste them into a fresh "Web Only" project shell?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: SAP BUILD - QUERY
entry.content: Is there any sap document for best practices for sap build and sap build products ?

entry.title: Failed to subscribe Build Apps in BTP Trial
entry.content:
Hi,

about a year ago the issue has been exactly described here: Failed to subscribe Build Apps in BTP Trial - SAP Community

There the answer was that it was fixed. Now again.

Anybody any idea:

- is SAP Build Apps still available in TRIAL

- does it still have the same product name

- anybody working on the issue?

Regards

Wolf

entry.title: Free Edition Migration Question for Existing App
entry.content:
Hi,

I currently have a production app built using SAP Build Apps Free Edition.

I understand that the Free Edition will be deprecated on September 30, 2026 and that SAP Build is the recommended path forward.

I have located the project export feature which creates a .zip.gpg file.

Could you please clarify:

  1. Can Free Edition projects be migrated into SAP Build using these exports?
  2. If so, will the app pages, logic, data resources and project structure be retained?
  3. Is there an SAP Build option that allows me to continue maintaining my existing application in a visual low-code environment?
  4. Or would my application need to be converted to React/SAPUI5 code or rebuilt?

I’m trying to determine the best path forward and whether I can continue maintaining my existing application after the Free Edition is retired.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation – Click Dynamic "Clear" Button Based on Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am developing a SAP Build Process Automation BOT for the Post Incoming Payments Fiori application.

The Open AP/AR Items table contains multiple rows, each with a Journal Entry value and a corresponding Clear button.

My requirement is to find a specific Journal Entry Number (e.g., 40000005) and click the Clear button in the same row.

Since the table rows are generated dynamically in SAP Fiori, I would like to know the recommended approach in SAP BPA to:

Identify a row based on a column value (Journal Entry).
Click a button in the corresponding row.
Handle dynamically generated SAPUI5 table elements.

Screenshot attached for reference.

Any guidance or examples would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: Looking for Insights on Kids Learning App Development
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently working at Dev Technosys and researching kids learning app development for an education-focused project. We're exploring ways to improve engagement, personalized learning, and progress tracking for young learners.

I'd love to hear from the community:

  • What kids learning app development solutions have worked best for improving student engagement and retention?
  • Have you faced challenges integrating analytics, user management, or learning progress tracking?
  • What features do parents and educators value the most in educational apps?
  • Have you worked with providers offering kids learning app development services, and what was your experience?
  • What factors do you consider when selecting a reliable kids learning app development company?

I'd appreciate any recommendations, lessons learned, or best practices from your projects.

Thanks in advance for your insights!

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone Licensing vs HTML5 Application Repository for External Users
entry.content:
We are currently using SAP Build Work Zone to provide access to our SAPUI5 application. However, we have observed a very high consumption of Connections from external users, which has significantly increased our licensing costs.

As an alternative, we are evaluating an architecture where the SAPUI5 application is hosted in the HTML5 Application Repository and accessed through a custom AppRouter, without using SAP Build Work Zone.

In this scenario:

  • Would access to the application still generate SAP Build Work Zone licensing consumption (such as Connections or Active Users)?
  • If not, which SAP BTP services and metrics would be considered for billing instead?
  • Is using the HTML5 Application Repository without SAP Build Work Zone a recommended approach for reducing licensing costs in scenarios with a large number of external users?

We have reviewed the available SAP documentation, but it is not entirely clear how licensing and billing behave in this specific architecture.

Any clarification, documentation references, or recommendations from SAP or the community would be greatly appreciated.
